Where we are
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/DJI_0193_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg)
## Let's build the future of clean energy, now
Our 2026-2028 Strategic Plan accelerates growth in renewable energy: with 15 GW of new capacity by 2028, we are moving toward Net Zero.
[ Find out more ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/mission-2040)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/mobile-usa-roadrunner-solare-photo-2.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg)
## Our projects
Explore the map to take a trip around the world and discover Enel Green Power’s renewable and sustainable energy plants.
[ Find out more ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/our-projects)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/Enel-141_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg)
## Enabling progress with sustainable energy
We enable electrification and deliver sustainable energy taking care of people and the environment. Our new mission.
[ Find out more ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/mission-and-soul)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/diversita-inclusione_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg)
## Diversity and Inclusion: Our Team’s Strength
Enel Green Power is a big sustainable energy family. Equal opportunity and inclusion are the pillars of our work.
[ Find out more ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/careers/diversity-and-inclusion)
[](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/DJI_0193_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg) Let's build the future of clean energy, now ![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/mobile-usa-roadrunner-solare-photo-2.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg) Our projects ![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/Enel-141_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg) Enabling progress with sustainable energy ![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/hp/slider/diversita-inclusione_mobile.jpg.resize.375.467.center.center.jpeg) Diversity and Inclusion: Our Team’s Strength

## We’re making the energy transition process happen. For real.
We manage approximately 68 GW of renewable energy capacity across the planet.
This makes us the world's largest renewable energy company.
[Find out about us ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are "Find out about us ")
##  Closing in on   
Net Zero.
By 2040 the Enel Group’s activities will have no net impact on the climate due to carbon emissions.
[Find out how](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/mission-2040)
~68  Gigawatts
Total renewable capacity
~20  Billion euros
Our investments in renewables 2026-28
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/in-operation-plants/venaus/venaus-banner.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)
## Synergy between sun and water: Venaus’ innovative and sustainable model
[Discover the hybrid power plant ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/our-projects/operating/venaus-hydroelectric-power-plant "Discover the hybrid power plant")

  1. [Countries Africa ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# Africa
## Africa
We have 16 plants, both wind and solar, on the African continent. Our installed renewable capacity is around 1.5 GW.
Parco eolico Africa ![Parco eolico Africa](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti-africa.jpg)
## On the path to the energy transition
Find out more about all the African countries where we and our 100% renewable energy are present.

  1. [Countries North America ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# North America
## North America
We have around 100 plants across the North American continent, including hydroelectric, wind, solar and geothermal. Our installed renewable capacity is around 11 GW.
Parco eolico Canada ![Parco eolico Canada](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti--stati-uniti.jpg)
## On the path to the energy transition
Discover all North American countries where Enel Green Power and our 100% renewable energy are present.
[ ![Monument Valley Stati Uniti](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-stati-uniti.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) United States Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/united-states "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Cactus al tramonto](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-messico.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Mexico Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico "Visit the Country Hub")

Where we are
  1. [Countries Europe ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# Europe
## Europe
We have over 900 plants across the European continent, including hydroelectric, wind, solar and geothermal. Our installed renewable capacity is over 24.1 GW.
Diga di Rochemolles ![Diga di Rochemolles](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti-europa.jpg)
## On the path to the energy transition
Discover all European countries where Enel Green Power and our 100% renewable energy are present.
[ ![Torrente fra le montagne](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-italia.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Italy Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy "Italy: Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Mulini a vento in paesaggio di campagna](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-spagna.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Spain Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/spain "Spain: Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Paesaggio di mare e montagne](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/grecia/grecia_768x400.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Greece Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/greece "Greece: Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Portugal](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/portogallo/scogliera_1200x630.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Portugal Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/paesi/europa/portogallo "Portugal: Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Fiume con mulino a vento](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-germania.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Germany Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany "Germany: Visit the Country Hub")

Where we are
  1. [Countries Central America ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


#  Central America
##  Central America
We are present in Panama, Costa Rica and Guatemala with 18 hydroelectric and solar plants. We have 0.6 GW of 100% renewable installed capacity and 61 MW under construction.
Centrale idroelettrica di Palo Vejo ![Centrale idroelettrica di Palo Vejo](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti-america-centrale.jpg)
## The path to the energy transition
Embark on a journey of discovery to all Central American countries where Enel Green Power delivers its 100% renewable energy.
[ ![Mare, spiaggia e palme](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-panama.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Panama Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Alba su paesaggio di lago e montagne](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-guatemala.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Guatemala Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Fiume nella foresta](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-costa-rica.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Costa Rica Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica "Visit the Country Hub")

Where we are
  1. [Countries Asia ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# Asia
## Asia
We have 5 wind farms on the Asian continent. This equates to about 0.1 GW of installed renewable capacity.
Parco eolico tramonto ![Parco eolico tramonto](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti--asia.jpg)
## The path to the energy transition
Come on a journey of discovery to all the Asian countries where we deliver 100% renewable energy.
[ ![Paesaggio di montagna con pecore](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-india.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) India Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/asia/india "Visit the Country Hub")

Where we are
  1. [Countries South America ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# South America
## South America
We have over 150 plants across the South American continent, including hydroelectric, wind, solar and geothermal. Our installed renewable capacity is around 15.3 GW.
Centrale idroelettrica di Guavio ![Centrale idroelettrica di Guavio](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti--sud-america.jpg)
## On the path to the energy transition
Discover all South American countries where Enel Green Power and our 100% renewable energy are present.
[ ![Montagne e ghiacciaio ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-argentina.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Argentina Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/argentina "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Mare azzurro](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-brasile.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Brazil Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Paesaggio di lago e montagne](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-cile.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Chile Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile "Visit the Country Hub")
[ ![Cascata nella foresta](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-colombia.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Colombia Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia "Visit the Country Hub")

Where we are
  1. [Countries Oceania ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)


# Oceania
## Oceania
We have 2 solar parks in Oceania. This equates to about 0.3 GW of installed renewable capacity.
Impianto fotovoltaico di Bungala ![Impianto fotovoltaico di Bungala](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/Article-media-full-width_contenenti-oceania.jpg)
## The path to the energy transition
Embark on a journey of discovery to Enel Green Power’s 100% renewable energy delivered in Oceania.
[ ![Scogliera e mare in Australia](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/pagine-continenti/hub-section-paesi/Hub-section-australia.jpg.resize.282.150.center.center.jpeg) Australia Visit the Country Hub ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/oceania/australia "Visit the Country Hub")

seleziona la lingua  EN 
Where we are
# A global footprint
##  Our global presence 
We are the world’s largest private operator in the renewable energy sector. Browse the map in order to find out in which countries we are present and learn about some of our power plants around the world. 
[Explore our power plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries#impianti "Explore our power plants")
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/paesi/continenti/landing-paesi/banner-mappa-country-hub-global_2420x960.png.resize.320.155.center.center.png)
## Our power plants
Localization, information and unusual facts: a journey around the world in search of the places where renewable energy comes to life.  

[Discover the pages dedicated to our power plants ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/our-projects "Discover the pages dedicated to our power plants ")

Where we are
# South Africa
## South Africa
The rainbow nation is greener than ever: we are united with South Africa in the journey to the energy transition.

## Our facts and figures in South Africa
A story of passion, green energy and attention to the needs of local communities, leading the country towards a future of shared value.

Plants 15 Wind and solar
  * #### GW 1.26
GW 1.26 Total installed capacity

Sustainability 60 Projects carried out in 2019 towards external beneficiaries

Where we are
# Zambia
## Zambia
We are taking our first steps to guarantee widespread electrification in Zambia while contributing to the country’s energy transition.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Zambia
A constantly growing share of renewable energy is providing a decisive push to the energy transition while guiding the country towards a greener future.
  * #### Plants 1
Plants 1 Solar
  * #### Total MW 34
Total MW 34 Installed capacity

# Morocco
## Morocco

A story of passion, green energy and attention to the needs of local communities, leading the country towards a future of shared value.

Plants 3 Wind

MW 810 Total capacity

# India
## India
We stand by India in its energy challenge: increasing generation from renewable sources, to guarantee access to electricity in rural areas and to support the country’s sustainable development.

Where we are
# Costa Rica
## Costa Rica
A country with great potential in the development of renewables. We support the diversification of the country’s energy generation mix.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Costa Rica
Renewable energy to sustain the country in its journey to the energy transition.
  * #### Plants 3
Plants 3 Hydro
  * #### Total MW 81
Total MW 81 Installed capacity
  * #### Sustainability 8
Sustainability 8 Projects carried out in 2021 towards external beneficiaries

Where we are
# Guatemala
## Guatemala
The country of the eternal spring is investing in sustainability and innovation: we support the country’s sustainable development effort with its own renewable energy capacity.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala#projects)

  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Guatemala
A 100% green electricity supply to power the country’s energy system.
  * #### Plants 5
Plants 5 Hydro
  * #### Total GW 164
Total GW 164 Installed capacity
  * #### Sustainability 20
Sustainability 20 Projects carried out in 2021 towards external beneficiaries

Where we are
# Italy
## Italy
We’re leading Italy in its path to the energy transition. We’re fielding a solid renewable capacity that we expect to boost in the coming years.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy#projects)
  * [Become a key player ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy#join-us)

  * [3SUN ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy#3SUN)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy#stories)

## Our facts and figures in Italy
A green presence throughout its territory, in order to achieve a 100% renewable, constantly growing, energy production.
  * #### Plants 687
Plants 687 Hydro, wind, solar and geothermal
  * #### GW 16.83
GW 16.83 Total capacity
  * #### Sustainability 131
Sustainability 131 Projects carried out in 2019 towards external beneficiaries

title-178 _3Sun_
## The Sun Factory, research and innovation in the heart of Sicily
3Sun Factory is one of the largest PV manufacturing plants in Europe. From the 3Sun 2.0 research and innovation project comes the latest-generation modules: the photovoltaic bifacial panel in Hetero Junction Technology (HJT).
[Find out more ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/innovation/3SUN-factory "Find out more")
![Pannelo solare bifacciale 3SUN](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/italia/Side-by-side-italia-3sun.jpg.resize.320.250.center.center.jpeg)

Where we are
# Germany
## Germany
Collaborations are underway for wind and solar: we’re placing our portfolio of technical excellence at the disposal of Germany’s energy transition.
  * [About ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany#about)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/germany#contacts)


## **Wind and solar to get the ball rolling**
We have several collaborations underway in Germany to develop and popularize [wind power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/wind-energy "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/wind-energy"). We’re also studying the chance to spread solar power throughout Germany.
Thanks to these assessments, we’re planning on building a solid foundation for future opportunities in the country. We’re placing all of our technical and operative excellence at the disposal of Germany’s energy transition to help the country reach its renewable energy quota objectives for the European energy portfolio.
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/banner-comuni/Banner-posizione-aperte.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)
## Would you like to work with us?
Help us create an even more sustainable world: we’re looking for professionals who are ready to face the challenge of the energy transition.  

[Find out about the job opportunities ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/careers "Find out about the job opportunities")

Where we are
# Greece
## Greece
Our subsidiary, which has been present in Greece since 2008, completed the sale of a 50% stake in its Greek renewable company to Macquarie Asset Management in December 2023. We will, however, continue to contribute to the sustainable development and prosperity of the areas of Greece where we operate, applying the Creating Shared Value (CSV) model, finding new business opportunities, solving social issues and being competitive while creating long-term sustainable value.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/greece#projects)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/greece#media)


## Greece: facts and figures
Towards a power generation mix that leverages the country’s natural resources.
  * #### Plants 66
Plants 66 Wind, solar and hydro
  * #### MW 566
MW 566 Total capacity

# Spain

Plants 359 Hydro, wind and solar

## Creating social and economic development for local communities 
We are proud to stand side by side with local residents, skilled tradespeople and landowners as we bring hundreds of jobs and millions of dollars into local economies through our renewable energy projects.

## Renewable energy in the USA and Canada: FAQ
Solar and wind power are playing increasingly important roles, both in terms of the environment and the economy, but many people still don’t understand how exactly they work. In these two sections we give detailed answers to Frequently Asked Questions.
icon
###  Facts about Wind Energy 
Communities across the US and Canada are taking advantage of clean, renewable wind energy to make our power supply more sustainable. Due to multiple factors, including new end-of-life innovations, state, local and federal regulations, investment in wind energy is growing.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-eolica/faq-wind-energy-usa "Find out more")
icon
###  Facts about Solar Energy 
The future is bright for solar energy in North America. The adoption of utility-scale solar is rapidly increasing as technology improves and becomes cheaper. It is estimated that solar will account for 30% of electricity generation in the US by 2030.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/facts-solar-energy-usa "Find out more")
icon
###  Facts about Battery Energy Storage 
As renewable energy continues to grow in the US and Canada, so does the demand to install utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) to our projects. Our ambition to accelerate the energy transition and reach America’s net zero carbon goal by 2035 drives our effort to install energy storage capacity at our sites.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/impianti-storage/faq-storage-usa-canada "Find out more")
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/italia/banner-contatti_2420x960.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)

Where we are
# Mexico
## Mexico
A solid presence, destined for sustained growth in the coming years. We’re standing side by side with Mexico on the path to the energy transition.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Mexico
Our renewable projects ensure carbon-neutral energy that fosters the country’s sustainable development.
  * #### Plants 19
Plants 19 Wind, hydro and solar
  * #### GW 2.98
GW 2.98 Total capacity
  * #### Sustainability 48
Sustainability 48 Projects carried out in 2023 towards 75.359 external beneficiaries


Projects 
## Here’s where our sustainable energy comes from
Close
Close
####  _*Of the 19 plants, 11 are owned by Enel and 8 are stewardships, where Enel holds a minority stake.  
Numbers reflect operating projects and projects under construction. _ _The number of projects in[operation](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#vtooltip) and those [under construction](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico#vtooltip) also takes into account the power plants that have been partially completed.

Help us create an even more sustainable world: we’re looking for professionals who are ready to face the challenge of the energy transition.  

[Find out about the job opportunities ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/careers "Find out about the job opportunities")

title-49
#### Mexico moves with renewable energy
Enel Green Power has promoted the consumption of renewable energy ever since it first became operative in Mexico in 2008. It has consistently focused its efforts on building and operating sufficient capacity to meet the needs of its multiple clients, providing not only clean energy, but also tailor-made options, alternatives to conventional sources and a positive vision of the future.
[Read it now ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico/mexico-moves-with-renewable-energy "Read it now")
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/articoli-a-firma/wind-power-mexico_2400x1160.jpg.resize.320.250.center.center.jpeg)

# Panama

## Our facts and figures in Panama
Sustainable energy that respects the country’s ecosystem and abundant biodiversity, while helping diversify its power generation mix.

Plants 12 Solar and hydro
  * #### MW 460
MW 460 Total capacity

Sustainability 13 Projects carried out in 2021 towards external beneficiaries

## Would you like to work with us?
Help us create an even more sustainable world: we’re looking for professionals who are ready to face the challenge of the energy transition.  

[Find out about the job opportunities ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/careers "Find out about the job opportunities")

# Blowing Art Initiative brings colour, pride and community connection to Impofu Wind Farm
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/blowing-art-initiative)

Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/storie/hero-Blowing-Art.jpg)
South Africa’s energy transition is a story of people and progress. Enel Green Power South Africa recently celebrated this ethos at the Impofu Wind Farm in the Eastern Cape, where we unveiled the Blowing Art Initiative, a creative project that turns the towering turbines into vibrant symbols of community pride and shared ownership.  

## Where art meets clean energy
Impofu is the largest wind project under construction by Enel Green Power globally, with 57 turbines that will soon deliver renewable electricity to support South Africa’s energy transition. Through the Blowing Art Initiative, these structures now have another, more socially driven purpose: telling the stories of local communities.
Under the theme “People are Energy,” three celebrated South African artists, Falko, Motel, and Bushy, painted colourful murals on the lower sections of selected turbines, bringing heritage and imagination to life. Each artwork reflects unity, cultural identity, and the promise of a future powered by wind.  

## A day of creativity and learning
With community participation a key part of the initiative, 12 learners from local schools were invited to join the artists on site for a day to add their own brushstrokes and learn about wind power in interactive sessions. For some children, it was their first encounter with renewable energy and a chance to explore creativity in a way that felt accessible and exciting. Through the experience, they discovered how a wind farm works and learned painting techniques from professionals, blending science and art into one immersive session that was simple, engaging and filled with possibility.
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/storie/gallery_1_Blowing-Art.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/storie/gallery_2_Blowing-Art.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/storie/gallery_3_Blowing-Art.jpg)
## Art as a bridge between people and technology
Art has always been a force for inspiration and connection. Combined with clean energy, this power has created a space for shared ownership and pride. The turbines at Impofu are now living canvases, telling local stories in full colour. Each time a blade turns, it celebrates progress and creativity. The murals also offer a welcoming touchpoint for future educational visits, where learners and community groups can experience renewable energy in a way that feels human and inspiring.  

## A promise of connection
Renewable energy in the country represents more than infrastructure—it serves as a platform for opportunity. The initiative invites artists and learners to leave their unique mark on the turbines, honouring local culture, uplifting community voices, and reinforcing a shared commitment to a just and inclusive energy transition. This approach forms part of a broader vision to develop projects that create value beyond electricity, seeing each wind farm as a chance to connect with communities, support education, and inspire creativity. The Blowing Art Initiative illustrates how technology and culture can work together to create meaningful social impact.
## Safety and sustainability at the core
The project was planned with a strong focus on safety and responsible execution. Access was limited to the lower sections of the towers, and the murals were completed using outdoor-grade, long-lasting paint to ensure durability. This attention to detail means the artworks will remain vibrant for years, as a testament to collaboration, connection and care.  

## The future looks bright
The Blowing Art Initiative forms part of a broader programme focused on local employment, skills development, and partnerships that deliver long‑term social value. With many turbines on site, the concept has the potential to grow, involving more artists and schools in the future. Enel Green Power South Africa is exploring ways to make this expansion possible, creating opportunities for wider community participation and allowing more stories to be expressed through art. The initiative also aims to inspire young people by introducing them to the power of wind and creativity, nurturing imaginations that may one day lead them toward careers as energy engineers or artists. Guided by the principle that “People are Energy,” the company positions the project as a commitment to keeping communities at the heart of the country’s renewable‑energy journey. By bringing art and technology together, the initiative seeks to cultivate opportunity and build a future shaped collaboratively with the communities it serves.

##  Creating a healthy environment at a rural school in Zambia 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/creating-healthy-environment-at-a-rural-school-in-zambia)

Link copied to clipboard
07 October 2025
# Creating a healthy environment at a rural school in Zambia
Pupils at the Shantumbu Combined School in Kafue, Zambia did not have access to modern hygienic ablution facilities or clean running water. They were obliged to use unsanitary pit latrines and had nowhere to wash their hands, but that all changed when the school got in touch with Enel Green Power.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/creating-healthy-environment-at-a-rural-school-in-zambia)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/creating-healthy-environment-at-a-rural-school-in-zambia)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/creating-healthy-environment-at-a-rural-school-in-zambia)


Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/sudafrica/zambia_2400x1160.jpg)
At Enel Green Power we are deeply committed to **serving the communities that host us** and to working toward achieving the **UN’s 17 Sustainable Development Goals**. In the case of our support for the Shantumbu Combined School in rural Zambia, SDGs 3 (**Good Health and Well-being**) and 4 (**Quality Education**), and 6 (**Clean Water and Sanitation**) are particularly relevant. According to [**UNICEF**](https://www.unicef.org/zambia/education), Zambia “has achieved near universal primary school completion levels,” but “transition rates from primary to secondary school continue to remain low at 67.5%.” For **girls at secondary level** , “there are also barriers around the low value placed by some communities on girls’ receiving a secondary education, teenage pregnancy, and child marriage.” Not only that, there are considerable **regional differences** regarding the country’s schools, and “**the quality of education also continues to be a challenge** , though national assessments have shown significant improvement in this area.”
## **Health and safety first**
For all of these reasons, our colleagues at the **Ngonye Solar Plant** in the Lusaka South Multi-Facility Economic Zone (LSMFEZ) in the Kafue District were delighted to help a local school. The Shantumbu Combined School caters to pupils from the ages of 5 (first grade) to 18 (twelfth grade), but it had a major problem: the students were obliged to use unsanitary pit latrines and had nowhere to wash their hands. 
The latrines posed **a serious health and safety risk** : they were used by pupils of all ages, thereby increasing the chances of spreading germs. Not only were the pupils exposed to many disease-causing pathogens, they also risked falling into the pit and sustaining injuries. In 2022, we therefore decided to replace the latrines with **hygienic ablution facilities**.
## **The local community**
We hired a local contractor, GIEVO Investments, which is owned by Grace Chanda, to build a brand-new bricks-and-mortar modern ablution facility for the school. Chanda’s company utilizes local labour and materials, such as stone from the local quarry, and it works within specific guidelines **to limit impact on the environment** , which is also a priority for us.
The project is aligned with Enel’s core sustainability mission of Creating Shared Value (CSV) and we are honored to have had the opportunity to provide this facility and uplift the community by impacting the lives of pupils and teachers at the school.
## **A challenging situation**
Building the ablution facility did not come without challenges, however. As the school is located in a rural area, with rough, uneven roads, it wasn’tt easy for contractors to **deliver materials to the school**. These materials also had to be locked in classrooms to prevent theft. Furthermore, we had to **liaise with the local herdsmen** , as the facility was to be built on **tribal land**. The herdsmen understood the desperate need for the school to have the new ablution block, and so they lobbied for us to build it.
## **Uplifting**
The ablution facility was officially handed over to the school in April 2023 – only a few months after construction began. At the opening ceremony, District Education Board Secretary, Belina Moono, said that it was **an uplifting experience** to have a modern ablution facility with clean, running water at a school in a rural area like Kafue.
Ensuring the delivery of quality education to children involves **more than providing teachers** , **textbooks and classrooms**. A healthy environment and access to hygienic ablution facilities not only curb the spread of diseases, but also give pupils and teachers **a sense of dignity,** as well as pride in their school. In this way we are building a better tomorrow: this is what **sustainable development** is all about.

##  When the circular economy and serving the community go hand in hand: our commitment in Africa 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/furniture-donation-cederberg-matzikama-aids-network)

  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/furniture-donation-cederberg-matzikama-aids-network)


Link copied to clipboard
09 June 2022
2 min.
# When the circular economy and serving the community go hand in hand: our commitment in Africa
Our recent donation of refurbished office furniture to the Cederberg Matzikama Aids Network, in Citrusdal in the Western Cape, is a good example of our commitment both to the circular economy and the communities that host us.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/furniture-donation-cederberg-matzikama-aids-network)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/furniture-donation-cederberg-matzikama-aids-network)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/furniture-donation-cederberg-matzikama-aids-network)


Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/sudafrica/circular-economy-africa_2400x1160.jpg)
Reducing and recycling waste is a priority area for us at Enel Green Power South Africa (EGP RSA) and is part of our commitment to the [circular economy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy"). If we can no longer use something, then, rather than throwing it away, we prefer to give it to another organization that can, especially if this is for a good cause. And that is why our Paleisheuwel Solar Plant, in the Cederberg Municipality, approximately 200 kilometers north of Cape Town, donated a large collection of office furniture to the Cederberg Matzikama Aids Network (CMAN).
CMAN is a local development agency located in Citrusdal, approximately 50 kilometers from Paleisheuwel. It has a strong focus on health and wellbeing, offering programs that relate to HIV/Aids, Tuberculosis DOT (Directly Observed Therapy), and food security community-oriented care. The donation included office desks, chairs and assorted tables. The new additions will make the CMAN office a more comfortable environment and enable greater collaboration in the workplace.
## **Sustainability is always the focus**
This project began as the result of a routine stock count of the various pieces of furniture that were no longer in use at the Paleisheuwel plant. The furniture was then collected, prepared and, where necessary, refurbished. This work was carried out by onsite service providers at the Paleisheuwel plant and our O&M team. 
> “Working at EGP, which is geared toward sustainability, the concept of the circular economy tends to permeate your thinking and perspective. So even when I’m not thinking about our work, I’m always considering ways to ensure we act in a sustainable manner. Once I took a tally of the amount of unused furniture at the Paleisheuwel solar plant, I realized that there was an opportunity to give it a second lease of life and we immediately began to think about what we could do with it to achieve the best outcome.”
> Tshepiso Mphelo – Plant Manager, Paleisheuwel Solar Plant Enel Green Power
Nor is this the first time we have worked with CMAN: we also built the Sandveld Health Centre with the Network. This fully functional, permanent clinic opened in 2019. Previously, people in Paleisheuwel could only access healthcare once every six weeks via a mobile clinic.
The medical facility - including its conception, building, naming and staffing - has been a model of creating shared value with the community. It stands as a testament to how an unsustainable situation can be effectively addressed through collaboration.
Sandveld has successfully brought significant improvements to the living conditions of the community of Paleisheuwel. This is primarily because it is more than just a clinic: the space is also used by various local groups that gather there in order to meet up, exercise and dance.
## **A welcome donation**
> “Your generous donation makes it possible for our non-profit organization to exist and furthermore enables us to offer much-needed services to those in need. Thank you again for your support and your commitment to our communities.”
> Christaan Oerson, Director at the Cederberg Matzikama Aids Network
We are committed to initiating and [supporting various impactful shared value initiatives](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/benefits-sustainability "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/benefits-sustainability") for the Sandveld Health Centre, CMAN and the greater Paleisheuwel community. Through this, as a sustainable energy supplier, we aim to be a force for positivity, enterprise development and job creation in the communities that host us.

Where we are
# United States
## United States
We’re a leading developer, long-term owner and operator of renewable energy plants with a presence in 9 states, working to build a sustainable future for communities and businesses across the U.S.

## Our facts and figures in the United States
Our innovative and sustainable projects are supporting the US’ clean energy transition.
  * #### No. of Plants 83
No. of Plants 83 Operational sites producing wind and solar energy, including hybrid projects and our standalone Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS).
  * #### Capacity (GW) 12.8
Capacity (GW) 12.8 Total installed wind, solar and BESS capacity.
  * #### Storage (GW) 1.4
Storage (GW) 1.4 Total installed capacity.


  * #### SUST Projects 211
SUST Projects 211 Community projects started in 2024 in partnership with local stakeholders.
  * #### US States 9
US States 9 We operate projects in 9 states, from Texas to North Dakota and beyond.
  * #### PPAs 115+
PPAs 115+ We signed over 115 power purchase agreements (PPAs) with utilities and leading commercial and industrial brands such as Anheuser-Busch, Meta, Google, T-Mobile, McDonald's, and many more.


_Numbers reflect official company Q3 2024 results_

## Renewable energy in the USA and Canada: FAQ
Solar and wind power are playing increasingly important roles, both in terms of the environment and the economy, but many people still don’t understand how exactly they work. In these two sections we give detailed answers to Frequently Asked Questions.
icon
###  Facts about Wind Energy 
Communities across the US and Canada are taking advantage of clean, renewable wind energy to make our power supply more sustainable. Due to multiple factors, including new end-of-life innovations, state, local and federal regulations, investment in wind energy is growing.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-eolica/faq-wind-energy-usa "Find out more")
icon
###  Facts about Solar Energy 
The future is bright for solar energy in North America. The adoption of utility-scale solar is rapidly increasing as technology improves and becomes cheaper. It is estimated that solar will account for 30% of electricity generation in the US by 2030.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/facts-solar-energy-usa "Find out more")
icon
###  Facts about Battery Energy Storage 
As renewable energy continues to grow in the US and Canada, so does the demand to install utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) to our projects. Our ambition to accelerate the energy transition and reach America’s net zero carbon goal by 2035 drives our effort to install energy storage capacity at our sites.
[Find out more](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/impianti-storage/faq-storage-usa-canada "Find out more")
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/storie/Rinnovabili-USA-2400x1160.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)
## Enel North America Inc. Anti-bribery policy
Enel North America, Inc. and its subsidiaries (“Enel”) are fully committed to the prevention of bribery and to conducting its business ethically and transparently. Enel has implemented and maintains an Antibribery Management System which is underpinned by our Anti-Bribery Policy.  

[Read the policy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/united-states/anti-bribery-policy "Read the policy")
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/italia/banner-contatti_2420x960.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)

##  Cookhouse: empowering women to celebrate african culture through fashion 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/empowering-women-to-celebrate-african-culture-through-fashion)

Link copied to clipboard
07 October 2025
# Cookhouse: empowering women to celebrate african culture through fashion
In South Africa a women’s rural community initiative has developed a sustainable and adaptable business model, thereby creating an inclusive and shared space where each generation can channel its knowledge and skills to generate income.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/empowering-women-to-celebrate-african-culture-through-fashion)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/empowering-women-to-celebrate-african-culture-through-fashion)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/empowering-women-to-celebrate-african-culture-through-fashion)


Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/Sophila-Women-Cookhouse-Primary-1_2400x1160.jpg)
In Cookhouse, a rural town in South Africa’s Eastern Cape province we are supporting the **Sophila Women Cookhouse Primary Cooperative** (SWCPC), which is located near the Nojoli wind farm. The cooperative brings together a generational mix of **women with diverse backgrounds** to channel their technical and creative skills into a sustainable and adaptable business model: the design and sewing of uniforms for schools and cultural groups, tracksuits, traditional and fashion clothing and soft furnishings. Not only does this initiative create jobs in a country with a serious unemployment problem, it also helps to preserve and promote traditional African art, culture and heritage.
The **Nojoli wind farm** , which was the first wind farm that Enel Green Powerhad in in South Africa has provided funding for **key technology and machinery upgrades** that have increased both production and efficiency. This has enabled the cooperative to service corporate clients and to continue to diversify its products. 
## **Unlocking potential**
The cooperative currently has five full-time employees, and has developed a reputation for producing quality clothing, while providing **a highly professional, customer-centric service**. It has become a shining example of fostering a sense of community through a collective vision. The cooperative is a space for both the young and mature women of Cookhouse to learn new skills that can lead to **greater self-sufficiency** and unlock new possibilities. This is a greatexample of the underlying potential for local communities to **create sustainable economic opportunities** and contribute to their development through entrepreneurship and innovation.
## **The challenge for women**
By working closely with local communities, we are able to understand their needs and concerns and develop projects that are aligned with their priorities. Unemployment is a major challenge in the country, where a national rate of 32.7% was recorded in the fourth quarter of 2022. Research by South Africa’s [national statistical service](https://www.statssa.gov.za/?p=16113) highlights that not only do unemployment disparities exist, but **women are also the most vulnerable** **people** the labor market. 
## **Empowering local communities**
We firmly believe that the **health and prosperity** of the communities in which we operate are linked to – and will impact – our long-term success. And so, instead of treating the social and environmental issues present in these communities as peripheral concerns, we think that helping to address them is critical to our business strategy and operations. As we continue **our journey toward the energy transition** , we will carry on building lasting and empowering relationships with local stakeholders.

Where we are
# Australia
## Australia
We are committed to supporting Australian companies and jurisdictions to reach their renewable energy and decarbonisation targets and accelerate the energy transition for Australia.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/oceania/australia#projects)
  * [Press Room ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/oceania/australia#press-room)
  * [Photo Gallery ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/oceania/australia#gallery)


## Our facts and figures in Australia
Our subsidiary entered the Australian market in 2017 with the acquisition of the Bungala Solar Farm. From here, we continued to grow our portfolio, aiming to significantly increase our installed capacity across wind, solar, storage and hybrid projects, we secured rights to a development pipeline of over 7 GW across Australia. In late 2023, we partnered with INPEX, who acquired a 50% stake in Enel Green Power Australia, strengthening our position in the Australian market. In late 2024, the company rebranded from Enel Green Power Australia to [Potentia Energy](https://potentiaenergy.com.au/), reflecting a new chapter for the joint venture entity co-owned by Enel Green Power and INPEX. While our name and brand identity have evolved, we remain fully committed to accelerating Australia’s energy transition and driving the potential for a sustainable future.
  * #### Operating Power Plants 10
Operating Power Plants 10 Solar and wind
  * #### Total MW 840
Total MW 840 Installed capacity

# Argentina
## Argentina

Where we are
# Brazil
## Brazil
We’re proud to have a solid share of 100% renewable energy in the Brazilian market. Our goal is to increase the country’s overall share of green energy, in order to guarantee access to electricity in rural areas while improving energy efficiency.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/brazil#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Brazil
A constantly growing share of renewable energy is providing a decisive push to the energy transition while guiding the country towards a greener future.
  * #### Plants 107
Plants 107 Hydro, wind and solar
  * #### GW 6.62
GW 6.62 Total capacity
  * #### Sustainability 182
Sustainability 182 Projects carried out in 2021 towards external beneficiaries

Where we are
# Chile
## Chile
In Chile, we’ve built South America’s first geothermal power plant. All our technologies are fully dedicated to supporting the country’s energy transition.
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/chile#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Chile
A solid presence to consolidate Chile’s role in the development of sustainable energy.
  * #### Plants 63
Plants 63 Hydro, wind, solar and geothermal
  * #### GW 6.9
GW 6.9 Total capacity
  * #### Sustainability 97
Sustainability 97 Social investment projects in local areas


Projects

## Would you like to work with us?
Help us create an even more sustainable world: we’re looking for professionals who are ready to face the challenge of the energy transition.  

[Find out about the job opportunities ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/careers "Find out about the job opportunities")

Where we are
# Colombia
## Colombia
We have 19 electric power plants with an installed capacity of 3.5 GW and five renewable power plants under construction. 
  * [Projects ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia#projects)
  * [Careers ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia#careers)
  * [Stories ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia#stories)
  * [Media center ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia#media)
  * [Contacts ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/south-america/colombia#contacts)


## Our facts and figures in Colombia
Projects acting as milestones in the country’s renewable power growth and a landmark in Latin America’s energy transition.
  * #### Plants 19
Plants 19 17 operating plants, 2 under construction
  * #### GW 3.99
GW 3.99 Total capacity
  * #### Sustainability 40
Sustainability 40 Projects carried out in 2019 towards external beneficiaries


Projects

Help us create an even more sustainable world: we’re looking for professionals who are ready to face the challenge of the energy transition.

##  Zambia’s sun-tracking solar panels are a first for Sub-Saharan Africa 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia/sun-tracking-solar-panels-zambia)

Link copied to clipboard
28 January 2022
2 min.
# Zambia’s sun-tracking solar panels are a first for Sub-Saharan Africa
We are using state-of-the-art technology for tracking the Sun’s path at the Ngonye solar PV plant in Zambia. This is the first time a system of this kind has been used in Sub-Saharan Africa, as we endeavour to provide stable – and sustainable – energy.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia/sun-tracking-solar-panels-zambia)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia/sun-tracking-solar-panels-zambia)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/zambia/sun-tracking-solar-panels-zambia)


Link copied to clipboard
Ngonye solar plant ![Ngonye solar plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/zambia/ngonye_2400x1160.JPG)
The 34 MW **Ngonye solar photovoltaic plant** is the Enel Green Power’s first power plant in Zambia. The [PV facility](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/centrale-solare "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/solar-energy/solar-plants"), which is located in the Lusaka South Multi-Facility Economic Zone in the country’s south, is part of the World Bank Group’s Scaling Solar program. This program is being carried out by Zambia’s Industrial Development Corporation (IDC), which in June 2016 awarded Enel the right to develop, finance, construct, own and operate the plant.
Ngonye solar plant uses tracking systems that feature [solar PV panels](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/modulo-fotovoltaico "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/solar-energy/photovoltaic-module") to **track the movement** of the Sun during the course of the day, **capturing sunlight** and converting the energy into electricity.
It is the only site in sub-Saharan Africa to use tracking technology developed by [Convert Italia](https://www.convertitalia.com/ "https://www.convertitalia.com/"), a Rome-based company that applies photovoltaic solutions all over the world. EGP decided to enlist the services of Convert Italia because the designated land for the solar farm was not large enough to accommodate the planned infrastructure.
## **GPS and other technology**
The tracking of the Sun is possible thanks to Global Positioning Satellite (GPS) connected to an Electronic Tracker Control Board (ETCB). The integrated GPS device acquires both the date and time. This information, along with astronomical clock algorithms, is sufficient to identify and properly track the Sun's position.
Each single axis tracker automatically tracks the Sun's East - West movement during the day via the ETCB. A single control board follows a maximum of 10 structures with a photovoltaic energy capacity of about 97.5 kWp. The primary benefit of the tracking system is that it **improves efficiency by increasing energy** output as it lengthens the plant’s peak generation period above that of similar sized fixed axis plants.
The Ngonye plant, which was commissioned in 2019, is a joint venture between EGP and the IDC. The operation has the capacity to supply 34 MW of energy to ZESCO, the national electricity utility, under an existing 25-year [Power Purchase Agreement](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/it/offerta/prodotti-e-servizi/ppa-fisico) (PPA).
EGP Zambia’s **Kachinga-Wankunda Phiri** says the plant is one of only two large-scale, grid-connected renewable energy facilities operating in the country. “To date, there has only been one procurement round in Zambia leading to projects achieving commercial operations – The Scaling Solar Program – which was managed by the IDC in partnership with the International Finance Corporation (IFC),” he says.
## **Sustainable solutions**
Due to the country’s lack of a long-term procurement program for renewables, EGP Zambia is focused on growing its commercial and industrial pipeline. “Our goal is to provide tailormade solutions to commercial and industrial customers that will allow them to reduce their carbon footprints, achieve their sustainability goals and facilitate business continuity by ensuring a stable energy supply,” Phiri says in conclusion.

##  How our game-changing bursary program is helping South Africa’s youth 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/our-game-changing-bursary-program)

Link copied to clipboard
07 October 2025
# How our game-changing bursary program is helping South Africa’s youth
Our bursary program aims to tackle youth unemployment by offering young people the chance to increase their educational opportunities and drive future change in South Africa.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/our-game-changing-bursary-program)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/our-game-changing-bursary-program)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/our-game-changing-bursary-program)


Link copied to clipboard
in Photo: Unati Renene, Principal of Gariep High School, Busisiwe Nohenda, Learner of Gariep High School and Bursary winner, Manuele Battisti, Country Manager at Enel Green Power South Africa. ![in Photo: Unati Renene, Principal of Gariep High School, Busisiwe Nohenda, Learner of Gariep High School and Bursary winner, Manuele Battisti, Country Manager at Enel Green Power South Africa.](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/ENEL-GREEN-POWER_PRIESKA-579_2400x1160.jpg)
In Photo: Unati Renene, Principal of Gariep High School; Busisiwe Nohenda, Learner of Gariep High School and Bursary winner; Manuele Battisti, Country Manager at Enel Green Power South Africa.
According to the United Nations, “[Education is a critical agent in addressing climate change](https://www.un.org/en/climatechange/climate-solutions/education-key-addressing-climate-change)” and that is why “**Quality Education** ” is number 4 on the UN’s list of 17 **Sustainable Development Goals** , or SDGs. But education is also critical in addressing many of the other problems that the world faces today: **poverty, inequality, unemployment and the health crisis** , to name but four. And in South Africa **the challenges facing youth** , who make up a third of the population, are immense. [According to Professor David Everatt](https://www.wits.ac.za/news/latest-news/opinion/2024/2024-04/south-africas-youth-are-a-generation-lost-under-democracy--study.html) of the University of the Witwatersrand (Wits) in Johannesburg, “Youth in South Africa have not fared well under democracy. They are the hardest hit by unemployment and the lack of opportunities, and show high alienation.”
## **Our mission**
For all of these reasons, we are actively promoting education in South Africa, with a particular emphasis on youngsters from the more disadvantaged communities where we operate. This is mainly done through **scholarships and bursaries** that offer young South Africans the opportunity to further their education. The latest example comes through our **Garob Wind Farm** in the Northern Cape. Specifically, on February 22 we organized a Career and Bursary Day at the province’s **Gariep High School**. This underscored our unwavering commitment to supporting the UN’s SDGs. Speaking at the event, our **Country Manager Manuele Battisti** reiterated our values of **trust, responsibility, innovation and proactivity** , adding that by putting people and the environment first, we can be confident about the future of sustainable progress in the country. 
“For EGP South Africa, sustainability is at the core of our relationships, from the communities where we operate, to our dealings with our partners and prospective clients. To this end, and to meet the 2030 Sustainable Development agenda, EGP South Africa recognizes **the importance of driving youth involvement** to meet these criteria,” he said.
## **Eligibility**
“Enel Bursaries” are available to tertiary students in any field of study, as well as high school students from grades eight to 12, and cover the **Northern Cape, Western Cape, Limpopo and the Free State provinces**. To date, the program has seen **177 bursaries** being awarded to students from these provinces. 
The bursaries for high school students cover school fees, stationery, textbooks, school uniforms and transport. In the case of tertiary students, they include registration and tuition fees and learning materials, as well as an allowance for meals and travel. Where applicable, they will also cover **university accommodation or residence**. 
Bursary applicants must meet certain criteria, with tertiary applicants needing to have completed their “Matric” qualifications, and must have been accepted by a university, or else they need to be currently enrolled at a tertiary institution, while those in high school must be in grades eight to 11. 
Furthermore, all applicants are expected to have achieved a minimum overall pass rate of 65%, and come from a household with an annual income of less than R357,000. **Candidates with disabilities are also encouraged to apply.**
## **We are not alone**
Local government officials and other stakeholders in attendance at the event not only showed their support for the host school, but pledged assistance in various forms. These types of assistance include **financial contributions toward the school’s needs** , as well as commitments such as providing accommodation for an additional student during their first year of tertiary studies. The launch of our bursary program stands out as **a beacon of hope and opportunity for the youth of South Africa** , particularly those from previously disadvantaged communities. **Our dedication to sustainable development and youth empowerment** is evident in this **life-changing initiative** , which not only supports academic pursuits but also **nurtures the potential of future leaders in the renewable energy sector**. 
“To quote a famous entrepreneur, sustainability is no longer about doing less harm, it is about doing more good. As EGP South Africa, we could not agree more and hope that these bursaries will enable our youth to do more good, empower them to realize their potential, and **drive positive change in South Africa** ,” Battisti said in conclusion. 
For more information, please visit <https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa>

##  Sewing the future of entrepreneurship in South Africa 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/sewing-the-future-of-entrepreneurship-in-south-africa)

Link copied to clipboard
07 October 2025
# Sewing the future of entrepreneurship in South Africa
Learning to sew clothes and uniforms, creating stable job opportunities for residents in an area of South Africa with high unemployment: this is the purpose of a project we have initiated in Adelaide, a rural town in the Eastern Cape, as part of our strategy for supporting communities near our power plants.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/sewing-the-future-of-entrepreneurship-in-south-africa)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/sewing-the-future-of-entrepreneurship-in-south-africa)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/sewing-the-future-of-entrepreneurship-in-south-africa)


Link copied to clipboard
Learning to sew clothes and uniforms ![Learning to sew clothes and uniforms](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/SudrafricaCH_2400x1160.jpg)
In **Adelaide** , a rural center near our Nxuba wind farm in South Africa's Eastern Cape province, the prolonged drought of recent years has had a very serious impact on the local economy. In this town of more than 10,000 people, unemployment has affected nearly 70% of the workforce.
In [**South Africa**](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa), we operate 12 solar parks and wind farms, and two of them (Nxuba and Nojoli) are actually in the Eastern Cape. But the value of our plants is not only measured in terms of installed renewable capacity. Sustainability also counts, as do projects that bring improvement to the areas where we operate.
Our projects often aim to reduce poverty in South Africa’s rural areas. It is precisely for this reason that the collaborative project with **D'Bongs Trading** , a small local sewing and laundry company founded by a female entrepreneur, **Ntombodidi Dilima** , came to life.  

## **Creating jobs with local businesses**
D'Bongs Trading has grown from 2014 up until now, thanks in part to its collaboration with Enel Green Power. EGP's support is in fact part of a broad project, primarily involving the supply of sewing and embroidery machines that have given the company the opportunity to grow and hire more staff. 
This growth led to a breakthrough when the company was awarded a contract for sewing covers for car parts with Mercedes-Benz’s East London plant, which became one of its largest customers.
"The sewing machine financing enabled us to get the Mercedes-Benz sewing contract: we see this as the creation of new business and an avenue for offering even more to our expanding customer base," Dilima says.  

## **Training in order to grow**
In order to encourage D’Bongs Trading's sustained growth, we have also supported specific training courses for its employees. The courses, which are run by specialists, have enabled the company to improve its skills and provide better services and products to customers. Now D'Bongs Trading is able to meet local needs with local resources, thereby boosting Adelaide’s economic development. They’re looking into the possibility of opening a shop for uniforms because learners currently have to travel about 150kms to buy uniforms, greatly assisting the community of Adelaide and Bedford
Supporting long-term entrepreneurship projects is part of our commitment to the areas in which we operate, where we create shared value with communities, in many cases providing training and materials, and revitalizing the local economy.

##  Smart glasses: how technical experts can monitor our sites from a distance 
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/smart-glasses-monitoring-plants-south-africa)

Link copied to clipboard
23 December 2020
3 min.
# Smart glasses: how technical experts can monitor our sites from a distance
Distance can present a challenge in a vast country like South Africa but, thanks to smart glasses, technical experts can help their colleagues solve problems at power plants hundreds of miles away. It’s a great example of smart technology.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/smart-glasses-monitoring-plants-south-africa)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/smart-glasses-monitoring-plants-south-africa)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa/smart-glasses-monitoring-plants-south-africa)


Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/sudafrica/realwear-glasses_split_2400x1160.jpg)
Smart glasses have revolutionized the activities of **Operation & Maintenance** (O&M) by improving multiple aspects: from the effectiveness of actions to the safety of our employees, from the use of resources to cost reduction. We have been using this technology at Enel Green Power for several years, and it has enabled us to follow work **remotely** at numerous renewable plants in different countries. This approach is now being adopted in [South Africa](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/africa/south-africa) in order to bring about a major step forward in terms of **efficiency, operational excellence, innovation and safety**. For this reason, RealWear® smart glasses are now being used at most of our operational sites around the country.
## **How they work**
The head-mounted devices clip into the safety helmets of users and allow for remote mentor video calling, document navigation and guided workflow, among other applications. 
> "The glasses are particularly useful on site for solving machinery-related issues. While our technicians understand the machinery and have been trained to troubleshoot most issues, sometimes they need additional input from an expert".
> Asante Phiri, Head of Maintenance and Technical Services, EGP South Africa.
“Because people with **expert knowledge** are often located in **Johannesburg** , Europe, or elsewhere – and sometimes at the site of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) – the smart glasses are an excellent way for them to gain sight of the machinery without having to travel all the way there,” Asante Phiri adds.
## **A helping hand**
EGP employees on site can liaise with an expert, who can then walk the technician through various processes or procedures and advise them on how to deal with the problem.
Asante says that, once connected, the person on the other side is able to see what the wearer is seeing via the camera on the glasses, while communicating through the microphone and headphones.
> “The glasses also have a liquid crystal display (LCD) that allows the wearer to see user menu items, as well as images and documents. In addition, they can record videos and take pictures.”
> Asante Phiri, Head of Maintenance and Technical Services, EGP South Africa
The glasses can be put to simpler use. “Sometimes the technicians out in the field want a specific part from the **warehouse** in Johannesburg. In some instances, they may want to check that the part in the warehouse is the exact part they require – and they can use smart glasses to do so,” adds **Asante.**
## **Virtual meetings**
The smart glasses need a **WiFi or mobile connection** in order to work. Applications like Microsoft Teams can also be downloaded, enabling the user to take part in team meetings.
EGP uses an interactive cloud platform, **Brochesia** , to facilitate the interaction. The difference between Brochesia and other cloud platforms is that its software is specifically designed to facilitate the interaction between the users in real time, without compromising data security.
Asante says that, while employees have reported that the glasses work very well, one of the **challenges** that needs to be overcome is sustained WiFi or 3G reception. “Because our sites are in some of the country’s most remote locations, internet accessibility can be an issue. We are, however, finding ways to overcome this, and we’re looking at installation of equipment to improve network reception.”
The glasses will have a significant impact on the way some aspects of the work are performed. “There is no doubt that the glasses will help **save time and money** – time the expert would have needed to travel to the site; and money that would have been spent on travel and equipment downtime,” concludes Asante.
Smart Glasses are a splendid case of state-of-the-art technology being used to help implement the smartest idea of them all: [renewable and sustainable energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili).

Where we are
  1. [Countries Mora Online, an app to revive the local economy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)
  2. [Central America Mora Online, an app to revive the local economy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)
  3. [Costa Rica Mora Online, an app to revive the local economy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica)


21 April 2022
2 min.
# Mora Online, an app to revive the local economy
In Costa Rica we are creating shared value with an app for mobile phones and tablets that enables vendors in the Mora canton to promote and sell their products and services.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/app-local-economy-costa-rica)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/app-local-economy-costa-rica)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/app-local-economy-costa-rica)


Link copied to clipboard
Nuestra energia mueve comunidades ![Nuestra energia mueve comunidades](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/costa-rica/mora-online_2400x1160.jpg)
After the hardships caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, we focused on putting technology at the service of small entrepreneurs, so as to **revive the economy** in areas near our [hydroelectric power plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-plants") in [**Costa Rica**](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica"). This led to the **Mora Online** app, a project developed by a group of Costa Ricans for the people of the Mora canton, so that they can buy products and services from small local businesses, in the comfort of their own homes, and with just one click.
The idea stemmed from an internal competition launched at a global level by Enel so that the Group's employees could come up with **solutions to remedy the various economic impacts** of the pandemic in the areas where we are present, providing resources like time, money, ingenuity and creativity.
This app for mobile phones and tablets was developed by the Municipality of Mora together with the Cantonal Union of Development Associations of Mora and Mora Unida. It helps users **access a wide range of local products and services** , covering categories such as: pet supplies, food, electronics, education, transportation, household products and professional services, among others.
According to **Luis Alberto Cerdas** , Head of Operations and Maintenance for Enel Green Power in Costa Rica and leader of this project, "the aim of this initiative is to promote [economic recovery by focusing on small entrepreneurs from the communities near Enel Green Power plants.](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/benefici-sostenibilita "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/benefits-sustainability")" The app already has more than 40 registered vendors and, as Cerdas explained, "the project can be replicated in other countries.”
This project implements Enel's [**Creating Shared Value**](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/creating-shared-value "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/creating-shared-value")strategy, as it seeks to transform corporate social responsibility into something even more meaningful, by identifying actions in the short, medium and long term, aligning the business perspective with the needs of local communities through concrete and recognizable initiatives.
