Where we are
  1. [Countries Central America has achieved the goal of 10 million hours without accidents ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)
  2. [Central America Central America has achieved the goal of 10 million hours without accidents ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)
  3. [Costa Rica Central America has achieved the goal of 10 million hours without accidents ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica)


30 September 2020
2 min.
# Central America has achieved the goal of 10 million hours without accidents
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-achieved-goal-ten-million-hours-without-accidents)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-achieved-goal-ten-million-hours-without-accidents)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-achieved-goal-ten-million-hours-without-accidents)


Link copied to clipboard
Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power ![Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/pannelli-solari-panama_2400x1160.jpg)
Staff members from all power plants and offices in Guatemala, Costa Rica and Panama have given a clear example of the Enel Group's safety-focused philosophy, by reaching the milestone of 10 million hours without accidents over the last four years.
This is the result of a planned program for occupational health and safety that features Continuous Training and the promotion of a safety culture involving all employees and third parties; Good Occupational Health and Safety Practices; Recording and Monitoring of lessons learned; Monitoring of Occupational Health and Safety Indicators and timely Identification of risks, among others.
“As a region, we are proud to have reached this goal. Nevertheless, this is a work in progress and a team effort that is strengthened every day with a solid safety culture, involving all of us, employees and third parties, and with the analysis of reported data and preventive decision-making,” says Bruno Riga, Head of Central America for Enel Global Power Generation.
Another essential element of our safety policy is the creation of shared value, thanks to close collaboration with all our contractors, and this has enabled us to constantly convey this safety culture, and not just in our plants and offices.
“Our strict safety standards and the application of technological innovations such as HSEQ4U are practices that have contributed significantly to reaching this milestone. The most important thing, however, is that everyone at Enel has embraced safety as a way of life,” says Juan José Ruiz, Head of HSEQ for the region.
It is also worth pointing out that our Stop Work policy gives all employees the right to stop work if they feel it poses a risk to their safety and that of their colleagues. This policy is a timely reminder that the health and safety of employees and the protection of the environment are part of our daily commitment, and they take priority over all other considerations.
Reaching the milestone of 10 million working hours without accidents offers us another opportunity to stress that everyone at Enel is responsible for maintaining the highest possible level of concentration. We must give priority to safety over everything else, while strictly observing the rules and regulations, and always being on the lookout for any possible risk, both to ourselves and those around us.
Congratulations to everyone on this achievement!

  1. [Media Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations at the country's first hybrid renewable industrial-scale power plant ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media)


07 October 2025
# Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations at the country's first hybrid renewable industrial-scale power plant
  * 

Link copied to clipboard
  * _Thanks to its location on the same site of Valle de los Vientos (90 MW), Azabache solar power project (60.9 MW) operates in conjunction with the wind farm to generate renewable energy from solar radiation and the wind._


**Antofagasta, August 18, 2022 –** Enel Chile, through its renewable energies subsidiary, Enel Green Power Chile, received authorization from the National Electric Coordinator to begin commercial operations at the Azabache Plant, which has an installed capacity of 60.9 MW and is also the first non-conventional hybrid renewable generation plant in the country, thanks to joint operations with the Valle de Los Vientos wind park (90 MW), allowing it to reduce its environmental impact and leverage more efficient electricity production cycles at more competitive prices.
"_This is great news for the country and for our company, since the authorization by the National Electric Coordinator allows us to finalize the development of the Azabache Plant, an important project within our decarbonization plan. This plant will help us inject an annual average of 184 GWh into the National Electric System, avoiding approximately 135,000 tons of carbon dioxide emissions. This milestone is aligned with the objective of carbon neutrality by 2040, and the fair energy transition implemented by Enel Chile,_ ” says **Fabrizio Barderi,** CEO of Enel Chile.
“ _At Enel, we are aimed at developing projects where we can implement innovation and be pioneers in seeking out new technologies and options that, above all, are environmentally friendly and respect the communities where the company operates. Our goal is also to make an ongoing contribution to the transition from a generation matrix based on fossil fuels to one based on 100% renewable generation,”_ adds **Fernando Meza** , Business Development Manager for Enel Green Power Chile.
Just 10 kilometers outside Calama, in the Region of Antofagasta, the Azabache Park is located in the Valle de los Vientos wind farm, where the distance between the windmills allows for enough space for a solar power park, where the use of trucks and shared facilities (like the O&M building or Substation) between the two parks to provide synergies in construction and operations. By incorporating Azabache into the National Electric System, this hybrid complex will be able to generate energy practically all day long, with a peak between 1:00 p.m. and 4:00 p.m., and in the case of Valle de los Vientos, the peak will also be in the afternoon when the wind picks up.
The Azabache solar power plant has around 154,170 two-sided panels, a state-of-the-art technology that can capture solar radiation on both sides of the solar panel to turn it into electricity, with the possibility of achieving up to 20% more generation capacity.
**Enel Green Power Chile** is a subsidiary of Enel Chile, leader of the country's renewable energy market, with a diversified portfolio that contains wind power (564 MW), solar power (1,537 MW), hydropower (92 MW), and geothermal power (81 MW).
### Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations at the country's first hybrid renewable industrial-scale power plant

Where we are
  1. [Media Guanchoi, Chile's largest solar plant, begins commercial operation ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media)


20 July 2023
# Guanchoi, Chile's largest solar plant, begins commercial operation
  * 

Link copied to clipboard
  * _Enel Green Power Chile's photovoltaic park is in the Atacama Region and has a net capacity of 398 MW.  
_
  * _Guanchoi will generate more than 1,100 GWh annually to power more than one million Chilean homes, avoiding the emission of around 900,000 tons of CO 2 into the atmosphere._


**Copiapó, 18 th July of 2023 –** Enel Chile, through its renewable energy development subsidiary Enel Green Power Chile, received official authorization from the National Electricity Coordinator to begin commercial operation of its Guanchoi photovoltaic plant, which, with a net installed capacity of 398 MW, and after successfully passing all performance and safety tests ensuring its correct operation, became the country's largest active solar plant.
This new plant is Enel's third largest solar plant worldwide, and thanks to its large size and net installed power, is expected to produce more than 1,100 GWh annually, 100% clean energy injected into the National Electricity System, which will power more than one million Chilean homes, while avoiding the emission of around 900,000 tons of CO2 into the atmosphere.
Guanchoi's construction included the installation of 893,508 solar panels with state-of-the-art bifacial monocrystalline photovoltaic technology, allowing for greater efficiency in capturing solar radiation, generating an average of 14% more electricity than conventional panels.
During its construction peak, the project had a workforce of more than 1,200 people, most local from the Atacama region. In addition, this plant's development considered implementing innovative systems and modern technology for such projects, such as incorporating wireless trackers to simplify operational maintenance: string inverters that provide greater efficiency for energy conversion processes and reduce the unit's maintenance costs.
**Enel Green Power Chile** is a subsidiary of Enel Chile, a leader in the country's renewable energy market, with a diversified portfolio that, as of March 31, 2023, comprises wind (715 MW), solar (2,043 MW), hydroelectric (92 MW) and geothermal (83 MW), totaling a net installed capacity of 2,933 MW.
### Guanchoi, Chile's largest solar plant, begins commercial operation

  1. [Media Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations of the new wind power park in Southern Chile ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media)


31 August 2023
# Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations of the new wind power park in Southern Chile
  * 

Link copied to clipboard
  * _The Renaico II wind power project, after passing all tests to ensure its correct operation in terms of both safety and performance, received authorization from the National Electric Coordinator to begin its commercial operations._
  * _With this authorization, Renaico II, located in the Araucanía Region and which has a net installed capacity of 144 MW, will generate around 516 GWh per year of 100% clean energy to be injected in the National Electric System._  



**Araucanía, August XX, 2023 –** Enel Chile, through its subsidiary for the development of renewable energies, Enel Green Power Chile, received official authorization from the National Electric Coordinator to begin the commercial operation of its Renaico II wind power park, a 100% renewable generation unit that, upon successfully passing all operations and safety testing, and with a net installed capacity of 144 MW, will begin to inject clean energy into the National Electric System.
The construction of Renaico II included the installation of 32 wind power generators with 4.5 MW of power each. These latest-generation units for the development of wind power parks allow for greater uptake of the wind profile available in the area where the project is located.
Thanks to its net installed capacity and the cutting-edge wind power technology used in its construction, Renaico II is expected to produce around 516 GWh of 100% clean energy for the National Electric System each year. This generation will help supply electricity to over 220,000 Chilean homes, while avoiding 407,000 tons of CO2 emissions into the atmosphere.
During the peak of its construction, the project had a workforce of over 524 people, mostly from the Araucanía Region itself, thus promoting the incorporation of local labor, their training, and the creation of shared value in the region.
This initiative, led by the on-site Enel Sustainability team, was carried out in coordination with the Municipal Office of Labor Information (OMIL) and the local neighborhood associations. At the same time, it forged ties with the Renaico Chamber of Commerce (CCR) to hire local services, strengthen the economy, and develop small businesses in the town.
**Enel Green Power Chile** is a subsidiary of Enel Chile, leader in the country's renewable energy market, with a diversified portfolio that, as of March 31, 2023, includes wind power (715 MW), solar power (2,043 MW), hydropower (92 MW), and geothermal power (83 MW), for a total net installed capacity of 2,933 MW.
### Enel Green Power Chile begins commercial operations of the new wind power park in Southern Chile

Where we are
  1. [Countries Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)
  2. [Central America Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)
  3. [Costa Rica Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica)


26 July 2021
4 min.
# Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER)
Enel Green Power Guatemala started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, establishing firm contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and with tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)


Link copied to clipboard
Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power ![Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/enel-green-power-mer_2400x1160.jpg)
Enel Green Power Central America established an **energy trading company** in Guatemala in 2020. Since then, it has consolidated its position as a **private leader in transactions** in the **Regional Electricity Market (Mercado Eléctrico Regional, or MER),** bycentralizing the group's trading operations. "This year we found opportunities to centralize our operations and set in motion synergies between the efforts that were being carried out in Panama and Guatemala. This was in order to channel the group’s strength and experience, through the new trading company we created," says **Percy Garrido, Commercial Manager for Enel Green Power Central America.**
The Regional Electricity Market allows streamlining energy purchases and sales among the region’s countries, in order to integrate and take advantage of the generation resources located in different countries. Enel Green Power currently has one of the most extensive contract portfolios, with numerous agents in the region. This enables it to carry out **coordinated transactions between Panama and Guatemala,** andto carry out transactions to and from different countries in Central America and Mexico. That means it can develop very special opportunities with its allies.
For this reason, **strategic and geographical advantages** can be gained from the countries where Enel is present, while a good position is achieved in the region's transactions. **Guatemala** , with 34% of firm power awarded, has managed to position itself as the **largest exporter,** with firm annual contracts with El Salvador, therebymaximizing strategic and geographical advantages and leveraging its position in the region.
## Years of experience
Although the group's trading company has been operating for a year, Enel began exchange operations to Central America from Panama in 2003. In the transition period prior to what is now known as the MER, it achieved, as a private generator, the largest transactions of the region in 2006 and over the last decade it has been the **leader in Panama in terms** of global transactions.
Enel Green Power Guatemala, on the other hand, started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, with exports through contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua. Since then, it has taken part in **Firm Transmission Rights auctions** , which have guaranteed its position on the transmission line to El Salvador. In 2019, Enel Guatemala started transactions of flexible physical non-firm contracts, which generate economic benefits for countries sending and receiving energy.
“In the specific case of Costa Rica, we are confident that opportunities for change will soon appear in the regulatory system, empowering private generators to export energy through the MER. This would enable us to put our experience in the Regional Electricity Market at the service of Costa Rica, so as to generate additional economic advantages for the country,” says **Percy Garrido**.
**Enel Green Power Central America** has been operating for 25 years in Costa Rica, 16 in Guatemala and 15 in Panama, with a total of nine [hydroelectric plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica) and seven [photovoltaic plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/centrale-solare), with a **combined installed capacity of 607 MW**. The plants are located in Costa Rica (three plants - 81 MW), Guatemala (five plants - 164 MW) and Panama (eight plants - 362 MW).
## Sustainable energy for a brighter future
The company certainly seeks to be first, but in a respectful and **sustainable manner**. “What makes our achievements so special and truly makes us proud is that the energy we contribute to the MER is 100% renewable, in alignment with the region's [decarbonization](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/decarbonizzazione) strategy, the group's [energy transition](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica) strategy, and in compliance with the United Nations’ [Sustainable Development](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile) Goals,” Garrido adds. Enel Green Power develops and operates [renewable energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili) plants all over the world and is present in Europe, America, Asia, Africa and Oceania. It is a world leader in clean energy, with a total capacity of around 49 GW and a generation mix that spans [wind](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-eolica), [solar](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare), [geothermal](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-geotermica) and [hydroelectric energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica). Enel Green Power is at the forefront in the integration of **innovative technologies** in renewable energy plants.
“In the region we are present with **solar and hydroelectric** technology and we hope to continue growing, supporting sustainable development in both Panama and Guatemala, which is why, in line with the upcoming long-term contracting processes in Guatemala and short-term ones in Panama, we are placing particular focus on more **open tenders,** with no technology restrictions, to promote competitiveness.”
## Energy Consultant
Not only does Enel Green Power Central America carry out transactions in the MER, but it has also consolidated its position as a **comprehensive energy consultant** for its customers. It offers them advice on consumption, smart metering, energy management systems (EMS) and networks of alliances that offer effective and quick responses, in addition to the support of I-RECs (International Renewable Energy Certificates). It provides an innovative vision for building a brighter future.

02 March 2022
2 min.
# Cleaner water in the Samalá River
Thanks to a circular economy project, almost seven tons of plastic be removed from the Samalá River. Not only did this help decontaminate the river, it also provided material for the construction of a community tourism center in the village of Chilascó, in Baja Verapaz, Guatemala.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/cleaner-water-samala-river)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/cleaner-water-samala-river)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/cleaner-water-samala-river)


Link copied to clipboard
Enel Green Power’s El Canada hydroelectric plant ![Enel Green Power’s El Canada hydroelectric plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/rio-samala_2400x1160.jpg)
The Samalá River is located in southwestern Guatemala. Its source is in the peaks of the Sierra Madre, in the departments of Totonicapán and Quetzaltenango. It runs almost 150 kilometers on its way to the Pacific Ocean, passing through hundreds of communities that use its water for farming and power generation. At the same, time however, the river also carries garbage and pollution from the wastewater of various communities.
**Enel Green Power’s _El Canada_** **[hydroelectric plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-plants") **is located in the river’s middle [basin](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/bacino-idroelettrico "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-basins"), in the municipality of Zunil. Ever since it entered into operation, alternatives have been sought to mitigate this environmental problem with a garbage extraction project. Waste is collected using a rake system and is then sorted and taken to a collection center where, on average, seven tons of plastic materials are processed.
The separation of this material created an opportunity to identify potential allies who could use the recycled raw materials to create new products, putting into practice a [circular economy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile/economia-circolare "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy") model. This provided the basis for a new idea in November 2021: to use panels made from plastic waste to build a community tourism office for the village of Chilascó, in the municipality of Salamá. This is in Baja Verapaz, and is home to Central America’s highest waterfall, _el salto de Chilascó_.
8,818 pounds of plastic will be used in building the office: 40% of this material will come from the _El Canada_ plant's collection center. That’s the equivalent of 15,000 medium-sized polypropylene bottles.
This project has helped promote a shift in production, construction and consumption models. This is in addition to promoting a more attractive, sustainable construction strategy, with more efficient timeframes and a more favorable environmental impact: 22 million liters of water will be saved for every ton of plastic.
Furthermore, as construction begins, eight temporary jobs will be created locally and employment opportunities will be generated for 90 families from the village of Chilascó. They will provide community tourism services, food, transportation and the sale of local handicrafts.
This project highlights the key role of circularity in promoting the [social, economic and environmental development](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/benefici-sostenibilita "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/benefits-sustainability") of local areas.

Where we are
  1. [Countries EGP Guatemala honored in 2020 Industrial Awards ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)
  2. [Central America EGP Guatemala honored in 2020 Industrial Awards ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)
  3. [Guatemala EGP Guatemala honored in 2020 Industrial Awards ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala)


14 December 2020
2 min.
# EGP Guatemala honored in 2020 Industrial Awards
Enel Green Power wins Environment award for waste management project, is finalist in innovation.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/egp-guatemala-industrial-awards-winner)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/egp-guatemala-industrial-awards-winner)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/egp-guatemala-industrial-awards-winner)


Link copied to clipboard
El Canada ![El Canada](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/el-canada-guatemala_2400x1160.jpg)
[Enel Green Power Guatemala](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala) has been honored in the Environment and Innovation categories at the nation’s **2020 Industrial Awards** in recognition of its outstanding performance and for being a source of inspiration for the rest of industry.
The award in the Environment category, presented by the Camera de Industria de Guatemala at a ceremony in front of 2,500 people, was for its project “Management of waste from the Samala’ River.” This involved the Hidroelectrico El Canada plant, one of four EGP installations in the country.
> "Winning the Industrial 2020 award in the environment category is an action that confirms the interest and commitment we have as a company in this matter, even the topic of Innovation is one of our four organizational values… All Enel employees are proud to belong to a company that is committed to the Sustainable Development Goals worldwide and proof of this is that it always seeks to improve the environment of the communities where it operates”.
> José Antonio Sánchez – Country manager
The waters of the Samala’ River in southwestern Guatemala are among the most polluted in the country, carrying quantities of solid waste from activities taking place in the waterway’s upper reaches. This interfered with the operation of the El Canada plant, with the winter runoff that passes through the upper and middle parts of the Samala’ River Basin, carrying all kinds of waste. Environment and safety policies, as well as sustainability, meant that this was a problem that had to be solved.
To handle the large amount of solid waste, EGP had to install a whole new infrastructure at the facility. This included a system of channels to take the waste away after removal, and a collection yard with a drainage system and waterproof floor to prevent leaching to the water table. A way of taking waste to the transport area had to be installed, and a storage site for the waste once it was collected and labelled also had to be chosen.
> "That the Guatemalan Chamber of Industry has taken us into account to participate in this category speaks volumes about the effort that Enel Green Power Guatemala makes to optimally manage the care of the environment in all our work sites and especially in the sensitive area, due to the level of contamination of the river, such as the El Canada Plant".
> Leonardo Tinti – Head of HSEQ Guatemala
The company reached the finals of the Innovation category and hopes to be able to take part next year with some of the projects it has already developed. It plans to show how creative and ingenious Guatemalans are when an interesting and ambitious mission is in play.
> "Being nominated in the Innovation Category was a great achievement for Enel Green Power Guatemala, taking into account that we have only been working under a structured scheme for two years, and one year with the Innovation Ambassadors Program. This nomination proves that we are moving in the right direction towards the creation of a true Culture of Innovation, and that we are already considered among the most innovative companies in the country".
> Desireé Díaz, Head of Strategic Projects

23 February 2022
1 min.
# The Classtainer: from container to classroom
This circular economy initiative provided the community with a classroom for preschool students by reusing a container that had served as an office at the construction site of the Chucás Power Plant in Costa Rica.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/classtainer-project-costa-rica)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/classtainer-project-costa-rica)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/costa-rica/classtainer-project-costa-rica)


Link copied to clipboard
Classtainer ![Classtainer](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/costa-rica/classtainer_2400x1160.jpg)
Preschool education is very important: it’s the first time that children are away from their parents. They also develop their first social interactions, as they are prepared for their entry into the school world. This is why having the right conditions for their education is essential. The “Classtainer” project came to life once these needs were identified and the concepts of the [circular economy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile/economia-circolare "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy") were put into practice.
This project involved taking a disused container, which had served as an office in the construction phase of the Enel Green Power Chucás [hydroelectric plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-plants") in Costa Rica, and refurbishing it as a classroom for two small public schools in the communities of Chucaz de Mora and Balsa de Atenas.
The Classtainer project has benefited preschool students, providing them with a safe space for their lessons, in addition to advancing the goal of considerably reducing waste through reduction, recycling and reuse activities. Furthermore, it has helped strengthen the relationship with the communities around the plant: it’s a clear example of how circularity creates [value for everyone](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/benefici-sostenibilita "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/benefits-sustainability").

Where we are
  1. [Countries Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)
  2. [Central America Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)
  3. [Guatemala Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala)


26 July 2021
4 min.
# Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER)
Enel Green Power Guatemala started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, establishing firm contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and with tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)


Link copied to clipboard
Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power ![Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/enel-green-power-mer_2400x1160.jpg)
Enel Green Power Central America established an **energy trading company** in Guatemala in 2020. Since then, it has consolidated its position as a **private leader in transactions** in the **Regional Electricity Market (Mercado Eléctrico Regional, or MER),** bycentralizing the group's trading operations. "This year we found opportunities to centralize our operations and set in motion synergies between the efforts that were being carried out in Panama and Guatemala. This was in order to channel the group’s strength and experience, through the new trading company we created," says **Percy Garrido, Commercial Manager for Enel Green Power Central America.**
The Regional Electricity Market allows streamlining energy purchases and sales among the region’s countries, in order to integrate and take advantage of the generation resources located in different countries. Enel Green Power currently has one of the most extensive contract portfolios, with numerous agents in the region. This enables it to carry out **coordinated transactions between Panama and Guatemala,** andto carry out transactions to and from different countries in Central America and Mexico. That means it can develop very special opportunities with its allies.
For this reason, **strategic and geographical advantages** can be gained from the countries where Enel is present, while a good position is achieved in the region's transactions. **Guatemala** , with 34% of firm power awarded, has managed to position itself as the **largest exporter,** with firm annual contracts with El Salvador, therebymaximizing strategic and geographical advantages and leveraging its position in the region.
## Years of experience
Although the group's trading company has been operating for a year, Enel began exchange operations to Central America from Panama in 2003. In the transition period prior to what is now known as the MER, it achieved, as a private generator, the largest transactions of the region in 2006 and over the last decade it has been the **leader in Panama in terms** of global transactions.
Enel Green Power Guatemala, on the other hand, started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, with exports through contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua. Since then, it has taken part in **Firm Transmission Rights auctions** , which have guaranteed its position on the transmission line to El Salvador. In 2019, Enel Guatemala started transactions of flexible physical non-firm contracts, which generate economic benefits for countries sending and receiving energy.
“In the specific case of Costa Rica, we are confident that opportunities for change will soon appear in the regulatory system, empowering private generators to export energy through the MER. This would enable us to put our experience in the Regional Electricity Market at the service of Costa Rica, so as to generate additional economic advantages for the country,” says **Percy Garrido**.
**Enel Green Power Central America** has been operating for 25 years in Costa Rica, 16 in Guatemala and 15 in Panama, with a total of nine [hydroelectric plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica) and seven [photovoltaic plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/centrale-solare), with a **combined installed capacity of 607 MW**. The plants are located in Costa Rica (three plants - 81 MW), Guatemala (five plants - 164 MW) and Panama (eight plants - 362 MW).
## Sustainable energy for a brighter future
The company certainly seeks to be first, but in a respectful and **sustainable manner**. “What makes our achievements so special and truly makes us proud is that the energy we contribute to the MER is 100% renewable, in alignment with the region's [decarbonization](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/decarbonizzazione) strategy, the group's [energy transition](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica) strategy, and in compliance with the United Nations’ [Sustainable Development](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile) Goals,” Garrido adds. Enel Green Power develops and operates [renewable energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili) plants all over the world and is present in Europe, America, Asia, Africa and Oceania. It is a world leader in clean energy, with a total capacity of around 49 GW and a generation mix that spans [wind](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-eolica), [solar](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare), [geothermal](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-geotermica) and [hydroelectric energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica). Enel Green Power is at the forefront in the integration of **innovative technologies** in renewable energy plants.
“In the region we are present with **solar and hydroelectric** technology and we hope to continue growing, supporting sustainable development in both Panama and Guatemala, which is why, in line with the upcoming long-term contracting processes in Guatemala and short-term ones in Panama, we are placing particular focus on more **open tenders,** with no technology restrictions, to promote competitiveness.”
## Energy Consultant
Not only does Enel Green Power Central America carry out transactions in the MER, but it has also consolidated its position as a **comprehensive energy consultant** for its customers. It offers them advice on consumption, smart metering, energy management systems (EMS) and networks of alliances that offer effective and quick responses, in addition to the support of I-RECs (International Renewable Energy Certificates). It provides an innovative vision for building a brighter future.

19 January 2022
4 min.
# Enel Green Power wins an award in Guatemala for its innovative solutions
The Chamber of Industry’s Award for Innovation was granted to EGP for the cutting-edge solutions at its hydroelectric plants: drones, smart glasses and an advanced data analysis system.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/industrial-awards-guatemala-winner-innovation)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/industrial-awards-guatemala-winner-innovation)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala/industrial-awards-guatemala-winner-innovation)


Link copied to clipboard
Enel Green Power Guatemala ![Enel Green Power Guatemala](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/egp-guatemala-premio_2400x1160.JPG)
For the past 16 years, the [Guatemalan Chamber of Industry](https://cig.industriaguate.com/ "https://cig.industriaguate.com/") has been giving [Industrial Awards](https://cig.industriaguate.com/premio-industrial-2021/ "https://cig.industriaguate.com/premio-industrial-2021/") at its annual Conference. It’s an opportunity for the Chamber’s member companies to shine and highlight their work as a source of growth and development for the country. For the second year running, [Enel Green Power Guatemala](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/guatemala") has been one of the event’s stars, taking home the prestigious award in the **Innovation category** for our commitment to [digitalization](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/digitalizzazione-energia "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/energy-digitalization") and our constant pursuit of cutting-edge solutions. At the 2020 edition we won the award in the Environment category, for activities carried out to [protect the Samalá River](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/es/medios/video/2021/10/salvaguardando-rio-samala-guatemala "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/es/medios/video/2021/10/salvaguardando-rio-samala-guatemala"), which feeds into the El Canadà power plant, one of our four [hydroelectric plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-plants") in Guatemala.
## **Improving processes and the way of doing work**
In 2021, it was our innovation initiatives, specifically those related to the digitalization of the four hydroelectric plants we manage, which won the award. The technologies that we have implemented are focused on safer and more efficient production processes, while improving competitiveness, security, environmental sustainability and the work of our colleagues.
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/guatemala-drone_2400x1160.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/smart-glasses-guatemala_2400x1160.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/guatemala-lavoratori_2400x1160.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/guatemala-lavoratori-centrale_2400x1160.jpg)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/guatemala/lavoratori-egp-guatemala_2400x1160.jpg)
## Smart glasses, drones and data
**Fernando Luna** , Manager of the Northwest region of EGP in Guatemala, explains that among the initiatives highlighted "we have implemented the use of smart glasses to carry out inspections, safety audits or visits to vendors and customers, thereby **saving time and resources** to make processes more efficient.”
Another innovation is the use of drones, as **Jairo Yac** , an Electrical Maintenance technician, explains: "With drones, we carry out inspections and create topographic maps that help us identify potential vulnerabilities in order to **prevent risks** for our collaborators."
Finally, as highlighted by **Eduardo Concinos** , a Hydraulic Works engineer, data is our ally in improving processes: “We have implemented a digital platform that helps us create a database of topographic, geographic, meteorological and hydrographic research, thereby enabling us to **map risk areas** for operations and speed up data collection processes.”
With this, "we have systematized our control processes using technological solutions that allow us to centrally control our four hydroelectric plants," says **Carlos Alconero** of Global Digital Solutions Guatemala, who adds: "Thanks to these technologies, we have digitalized various processes, such as work permits, inventory control, recording and monitoring biodiversity and routine checks at our [dams](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/diga "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/dams"), among others.”
Innovation is therefore at the service of our plants, starting with the safety of our people and the efficiency of our plants to protect the environment. This is because [innovation](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/innovation "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/innovation") is our vision.

  2. [Central America Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER) ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america)

26 July 2021
4 min.
# Enel Green Power Central America, a private leader in transactions in Central America’s regional electricity market (MER)
Enel Green Power Guatemala started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, establishing firm contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and with tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/central-america-mer-leader-transactions)


Link copied to clipboard
Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power ![Pannelli solari - Enel Green Power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/enel-green-power-mer_2400x1160.jpg)
Enel Green Power Central America established an **energy trading company** in Guatemala in 2020. Since then, it has consolidated its position as a **private leader in transactions** in the **Regional Electricity Market (Mercado Eléctrico Regional, or MER),** bycentralizing the group's trading operations. "This year we found opportunities to centralize our operations and set in motion synergies between the efforts that were being carried out in Panama and Guatemala. This was in order to channel the group’s strength and experience, through the new trading company we created," says **Percy Garrido, Commercial Manager for Enel Green Power Central America.**
The Regional Electricity Market allows streamlining energy purchases and sales among the region’s countries, in order to integrate and take advantage of the generation resources located in different countries. Enel Green Power currently has one of the most extensive contract portfolios, with numerous agents in the region. This enables it to carry out **coordinated transactions between Panama and Guatemala,** andto carry out transactions to and from different countries in Central America and Mexico. That means it can develop very special opportunities with its allies.
For this reason, **strategic and geographical advantages** can be gained from the countries where Enel is present, while a good position is achieved in the region's transactions. **Guatemala** , with 34% of firm power awarded, has managed to position itself as the **largest exporter,** with firm annual contracts with El Salvador, therebymaximizing strategic and geographical advantages and leveraging its position in the region.
## Years of experience
Although the group's trading company has been operating for a year, Enel began exchange operations to Central America from Panama in 2003. In the transition period prior to what is now known as the MER, it achieved, as a private generator, the largest transactions of the region in 2006 and over the last decade it has been the **leader in Panama in terms** of global transactions.
Enel Green Power Guatemala, on the other hand, started operating in the world of transactions in 2017, with exports through contracts with El Salvador, Honduras and tests carried out in Mexico and Nicaragua. Since then, it has taken part in **Firm Transmission Rights auctions** , which have guaranteed its position on the transmission line to El Salvador. In 2019, Enel Guatemala started transactions of flexible physical non-firm contracts, which generate economic benefits for countries sending and receiving energy.
“In the specific case of Costa Rica, we are confident that opportunities for change will soon appear in the regulatory system, empowering private generators to export energy through the MER. This would enable us to put our experience in the Regional Electricity Market at the service of Costa Rica, so as to generate additional economic advantages for the country,” says **Percy Garrido**.
**Enel Green Power Central America** has been operating for 25 years in Costa Rica, 16 in Guatemala and 15 in Panama, with a total of nine [hydroelectric plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/centrale-idroelettrica) and seven [photovoltaic plants](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/centrale-solare), with a **combined installed capacity of 607 MW**. The plants are located in Costa Rica (three plants - 81 MW), Guatemala (five plants - 164 MW) and Panama (eight plants - 362 MW).
## Sustainable energy for a brighter future
The company certainly seeks to be first, but in a respectful and **sustainable manner**. “What makes our achievements so special and truly makes us proud is that the energy we contribute to the MER is 100% renewable, in alignment with the region's [decarbonization](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/decarbonizzazione) strategy, the group's [energy transition](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica) strategy, and in compliance with the United Nations’ [Sustainable Development](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile) Goals,” Garrido adds. Enel Green Power develops and operates [renewable energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili) plants all over the world and is present in Europe, America, Asia, Africa and Oceania. It is a world leader in clean energy, with a total capacity of around 49 GW and a generation mix that spans [wind](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-eolica), [solar](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare), [geothermal](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-geotermica) and [hydroelectric energy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica). Enel Green Power is at the forefront in the integration of **innovative technologies** in renewable energy plants.
“In the region we are present with **solar and hydroelectric** technology and we hope to continue growing, supporting sustainable development in both Panama and Guatemala, which is why, in line with the upcoming long-term contracting processes in Guatemala and short-term ones in Panama, we are placing particular focus on more **open tenders,** with no technology restrictions, to promote competitiveness.”
## Energy Consultant
Not only does Enel Green Power Central America carry out transactions in the MER, but it has also consolidated its position as a **comprehensive energy consultant** for its customers. It offers them advice on consumption, smart metering, energy management systems (EMS) and networks of alliances that offer effective and quick responses, in addition to the support of I-RECs (International Renewable Energy Certificates). It provides an innovative vision for building a brighter future.

10 May 2022
2 min.
# Sowing the "Seeds of Knowledge" in the communities of Panama
The second edition of the "Seeds of Knowledge" project is underway. The initiative, which we set up together with Fundación Ciudad del Saber (The City of Knowledge Foundation) and the Imaginari Foundation, endeavors to teach new skills to small entrepreneurs, for the sustainable development of their activities.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/training-enterpreneurs-panama-second-edition)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/training-enterpreneurs-panama-second-edition)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/training-enterpreneurs-panama-second-edition)


Link copied to clipboard
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/panama/semillas-de-conocimiento-news_2400x1160.jpg)
We are continuing with our training program which is focused on promoting sustainable development for small farmers and entrepreneurs in the communities where we are present. “[**Seeds of Knowledge**](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media/press/2021/12/seeds-of-knowledge-initiative-for-the-formation-of-sustainable-communities)” is an initiative to **[Create Shared Value](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/creating-shared-value "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/creating-shared-value")** that has helped over **100 entrepreneurs (65% of which are women)** in the Chiriquí and Coclé areas. The project began as a public-private partnership, together with Ciudad del Saber Foundation, the Imaginari Foundation and local authorities, to develop projects focused on creating agribusiness and ecotourism services.
This first training session, which was part of the program’s second edition, was held at the Panama’s Ministry of the Environment’s (_MiAmbiente_) Center for Sustainable Environmental Development (CEDESAM, Centro de Desarrollo Sostenible Ambiental). Those taking part included local artisans and women at risk of social exclusion. The event focused on helping them develop their small businesses, thereby enabling them to support their families.
These workshops are designed to promote **community business development** , providing theoretical and practical knowledge to improve the skills and aptitudes of those taking part. This will enable them to generate profits and grow their businesses in a sustainable and competitive way. The issues covered in the course therefore include: individual and collective empowerment, understanding the market and how to adapt to it, creativity and innovation, entrepreneurship and general business development.
## **Green entrepreneurship**
In line with our global strategy to promote the [sustainable development](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development") of local communities, this initiative also supports the creation of **green businesses**. That means entrepreneurship focused on good practices for the use and conservation of valuable ecosystems, and which contribute to the [decarbonization](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/transizione-energetica/decarbonizzazione "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energy-transition/decarbonization") and **climate action** objectives that our company pursues.
The course was also attended by a group from the Jagüito community, who are developing a cooperative that will **plant 15,000 seedlings** throughout the province of Coclé, one of the areas most impacted by **deforestation**.
Furthermore, the workshop’s training activities also focused on managing a community nursery to create tree barriers to **mitigate the floods** that impact the Barú area, in the province of Chiriquí.
## **Long-term community benefits**
The goal of the training course is to create value for the communities that will persist through the development of new activities with business models designed to survive over time. Our initiative will continue to support entrepreneurs through the **first two years** of business development. This will give them time to formally establish their activities and obtain all the permits (such as those related to health, in the case of food items) required to sell their products in new markets.
When a seed is planted, it must be watched over every single day, in order to make sure it is growing healthily. Similarly, we want to create shared value and strengthen our links with the communities by promoting the involvement of all stakeholders in the areas where we operate.

16 February 2022
1 min.
# Solar Booths, a circular economy project for extending the useful life of photovoltaic panels
At the Jagüito and Esperanza plants, Enel Green Power Panama installed two security booths equipped with solar panels that had been discarded during the construction of the parks.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/solar-booths-project-extend-lifetime-panels)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/solar-booths-project-extend-lifetime-panels)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/solar-booths-project-extend-lifetime-panels)


Link copied to clipboard
Solar panels ![Solar panels](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/panama/casetas-solares_2400x1160.jpg)
The aim of the **Solar Booths** initiative was to provide security guards at the power plants with an appropriate workplace equipped with the basic services needed to carry out their work effectively and safely, in line with the Enel Group's Human Rights policy. The project was launched at the [Jagüito and Esperanza](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media/press/2021/12/enel-green-power-panama-inaugurates-two-solar-plants-jaguito-cocle-esperanza-chiriqui "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/media/press/2021/12/enel-green-power-panama-inaugurates-two-solar-plants-jaguito-cocle-esperanza-chiriqui") plants in Panama, located respectively in the provinces of Coclé and Chiriquí.
In accordance with the principles of the **[circular economy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economyhttps://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy")** and applying the Enel Green Power [Sustainable Worksite](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/articles/2018/09/egp-sustainable-worksites-in-the-world "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/articles/2018/09/egp-sustainable-worksites-in-the-world") model, **two security booths** were installed using **solar panels that had been discarded** during the construction work, due to minor imperfections. This is a perfect example of using good practices to **extend the useful life of**[**our assets**](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/sustainable-materials-management "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/who-we-are/sustainable-materials-management"). The feasibility of such actions is currently being assessed at other construction sites in Panama, as well as in other countries where EGP is present.
Furthermore (again according to circular and sustainable logic), other leftover construction materials were used to manufacture a **rainwater harvesting system**. The rainwater collected is used in the plant’s bathrooms and sinks.
These actions show that circularity can generate benefits, not only for Enel, but also for the environment, the local community and other stakeholders, in addition to generating significant savings from the reuse of materials and natural resources.

Where we are
  1. [Countries New life grows on the shores of Fortuna ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries)

08 October 2021
3 min.
# New life grows on the shores of Fortuna
Thanks to its circular economy logic, an innovative reforestation project enabled us, between 2015 and 2020, to restore and protect plant life on the shores of the reservoir of a Panamanian hydroelectric power station.
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/reforestation-project-panama-chiriqui)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/reforestation-project-panama-chiriqui)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/central-america/panama/reforestation-project-panama-chiriqui)


Link copied to clipboard
Riforestazione Panama - Enel Green Power ![Riforestazione Panama - Enel Green Power](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/articoli/news/panama-riforestazione_2400x1160.jpg)
Weather phenomena (such as the wind, rain and the rise and fall of water levels in rivers) and human activities can erode the soil and therefore damage the plants growing on the shores of reservoirs, and the banks of rivers or streams. This weakens the forest cover that protects the soil. In order to reverse this process, Enel Green Power Panama has for years been working on an **innovative reforestation project** to control the areas which are directly impacted by the erosion (or that are otherwise potentially unstable) along the shores of the reservoir of the **[Fortuna hydroelectric plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/articles/2021/01/fortuna-sustainable-hydroelectric-plant "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/articles/2021/01/fortuna-sustainable-hydroelectric-plant")** , which is fed by the Chiriquí River.[](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/stories/articles/2021/01/fortuna-sustainable-hydroelectric-plant)
It's a task that involves a number of measures aimed at protecting, preventing or promptly correcting any ecosystemic problems, while always focusing on safety. There are several consequences of soil degradation in these areas: from soil infertility, to the increase in the level of sedimentation in the reservoir or [water basins](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-idroelettrica/bacino-idroelettrico "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/renewable-energies/hydroelectric-energy/hydroelectric-basins"). Mitigating these impacts is always a major challenge from an environmental perspective.
## **A look at the reforestation process**
The Fortuna [power plant](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/energie-rinnovabili/energia-solare/centrale-solare) reservoir spans 1,000 hectares covered with water when the level is at maximum capacity (1,050 meters above sea level) and 100 hectares at its minimum level (1,000 meters above sea level). Furthermore, the area is also subject to strong winds, which cause waves that impact the bare soil and increase erosion.
For this reason, one of the measures implemented to reverse the process **was to sow and regrow Vetiver** (or Chrysopogon zizanioides), a type of perennial grass with tall stems and long, thin, hard leaves, and roots that quickly grow to four meters deep. Precisely because of the latter, Vetiver is famous for its capacity to combat erosion. For the sowing process, which was carried out in collaboration with the local community, with activities that involved clearing, replanting, fertilizing and pruning shrubs, our HSEQ (Health, Safety, Environment and Quality) department developed a nursery for this particular plant species.
Another strategy for stopping erosion involved placing **dead contour barriers** to reinforce the soil. It entailed using 44 pipes of 1.50 meters in diameter and 6.10 meters in length. The pipes had originally been discarded due to damage during the power plant’s transfer project. Following the principles of the **[circular economy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sviluppo-sostenibile/economia-circolare "https://www.enelgreenpower.com/learning-hub/sustainable-development/circular-economy")** , this waste was transformed into material that helped counteract soil degradation.
The results of these measures have been positive: between 2015 and 2020, more than 1,700 meters of dead contour barriers were built, as were more than 23,000 meters of live contour barriers. Vetiver grass was used for controlling the degradation of the lake´s shores, and approximately 2,300 meters of plant cover was restored using the same species around the reservoir. One example is the reforestation of one of the 17 tributaries of the Chiriquí River: Frank Creek.
## **Sustainability, a sign of identity**
This reforestation process is a fine example of sustainability. It’s based on circular economy principles and it has managed, thanks to **an innovative and creative idea,** to control the degradation of the soil around the Fortuna power plant reservoir. The corrosion is evident in the low levels of sedimentation highlighted by the periodic measurements carried out as part of the water quality monitoring process, and by bathymetry (a technique for measuring the depth of ocean and lake floors – ed). These measurements are made by the reservoir´s technical staff every two years, and they compare the baseline and the forecast of the sediment input to the reservoir with the results obtained by the afore-mentioned bathymetry.
Once again, sustainability proves to be a key element that spans every aspect of our business, and together with innovation and creativity, enables us to develop successful solutions for facing evolving challenges.

# Energy open house
## Energy open house
Doors open to the public: Discover all the chances to see Enel Green Power’s renewable power plants up close
  * [Open plants ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy/energy-open-house#1)
  * [Museum of Geothermics ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy/energy-open-house#2)
  * [Plants in Italy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy/energy-open-house#3)


## **Enter the heart of sustainability**
There are many ways to get better acquainted with the world of sustainable energy. The most exciting part is getting to the heart of the places that produce it: **guided tours of our plants** and the **Museum of Geothermics** are two distinct experiences that Enel Green Power offers to everyone - young and old, families and students - to learn more about renewable energy.
Discover more about the two formats that invite you to cross the threshold of our power plants and the Larderello Museum.
Open plants 
## Into the heart of hydropower generation
### [Plants in the Ossola Valley In collaboration with the Ente di Gestione delle Aree Protette dell'Ossola (EGAPO - Ossola Protected Areas Management Authority), we’re opening up our hydropower plants in the Ossola Valley, Piedmont, to provide a close-up experience of how hydropower works. Find out more  ](https://www.areeprotetteossola.it/it/attivita-e-outdoor/educazione-ambientale/2190-visite-alle-centrali-enel-dell-ossola)
### [Entracque power plant It’s now possible to get a close-up view of Italy's largest hydropower plant, either by visiting the Enel Green Power Information Center, located at the plant, in the municipality of Entracque, or by requesting to go on a guided tour of the power plant. Find out more  ](https://www.parcoalpimarittime.it/visit/centri-visita-musei-giardini/centrale-idroelettrica-enel-green-power-einaudi)
### [Edolo power plant In collaboration with the non-profit cooperative InExodus, we are opening our Edolo power plant for guided tours. In this way people can find out, close up, how water energy works. Find out more  ](https://www.inexodus.it/visite-guidate/centrale-enel-di-edolo)
### [Trezzo power plant Our Trezzo plant is a production facility, but also a cultural hub open to the local community. In collaboration with the Trezzo Pro Loco, visitors can tour the plant through multimedia exhibits designed for both children and adults.  Find out more  ](https://www.prolocotrezzo.com/website/discover.aspx?read=CENTRALE-IDROELETTRICA-CRESPI-VILLAGGIO-TREZZO-TACCANI-VISITE-SCUOLE&readcat=17)
Museum of Geothermics 
## Delve into the heart of energy
Our **plants** are not only centers of energy production but also places of culture and scientific dissemination. Come and discover the **Museum of Geothermics in Tuscany** , a multimedia gallery that explains this type of phenomenon and its industrial developments.   

### [Museum of Geothermics The interactive, high-tech, multimedia museum exhibition in Larderello (Pisa) allows visitors to learn about the technical and industrial applications and the evolution of geothermal energy. Find out more  ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy/energy-open-house/larderello-geothermics-museum)
![](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/immagini/country-hub/italia/centrali-aperte/Banner-country-hub-italia.jpg.resize.320.155.center.center.jpeg)
## Country Hub Italy
Enel Green Power is present in Italy with hydroelectric, wind, photovoltaic, geothermal and biomass plants. It currently has a total managed capacity of over 14 GW in the country.  

[Visit the Country Hub Italy ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/Italy "Visit the Country Hub Italy")

Where we are
# Rent out or sell your land for the construction of renewable energy plants
##  Rent out or sell your land for the construction of renewable energy plants 
We are looking for rural landowners in order to develop new wind and solar projects in Spain. 
[How it works](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/spain/owners#come-funziona "How it works") [Contact us](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/spain/owners#contattaci "Contact us")
  * [How it works ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/spain/owners#come-funziona)
  * [Contact us ](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/europe/spain/owners#contattaci)


## How does it work?
icon ![Baloon](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/icons/green/customer_chat.svg)
###  Step 1 
Fill out the form at the bottom of this page specifying the approximate location and size of your land. We will then study the technical feasibility of developing a renewable plant on your property.
icon ![Telephone](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/icons/green/telephone_assistance.svg)
###  Step 2 
You will be contacted by our Business Development department in Spain to begin the next stages of the process, which involve verifying the resource and its technical or environmental feasibility.
icon ![Leaf](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/content/dam/enel-egp/icons/green/environment.svg)
###  Step 3 
Join us and contribute to the sustainable development of our country and to the protection of the environment.
## **An opportunity to help the environment by renting out or selling rural property**
For further information contact our Business Development team by filling out the form below:

*required

Your data will be processed according to the website [privacy policy](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/privacy-policy.html) to answer your request. EGP is present in different Countries and, depending on the Country you specify in the contact form, your request can be redirected to the local commercial team, which will act in compliance with the applicable data protection laws. For California residents, please refer also to our [Notice at Collection](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/privacy-policy-united-states-california.html).

11 September 2020
3 min.
# Enel Green Power Mexico becomes the first renewable energy company in the Americas to certify its road safety management system
_Enel Green Power Mexico becomes the first renewable energy company in the Americas to certify its road safety management system with ISO 39001 standard._
  * [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico/iso-39001-standard-certification)
  * [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico/iso-39001-standard-certification)
  * [](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico/iso-39001-standard-certification)


Link copied to clipboard
# Media player
0:00Speed: 1x
Enel Green Power México has received **ISO 39001 standard certification**. This is one of the least known but most important forms of certification when it comes to the standards that a company must meet in order to adopt a Road Safety Management System.
With this, [Mexico](https://www.enelgreenpower.com/countries/north-america/mexico) becomes the first country where the Enel Group is present to obtain this type of certification. It sets a worldwide precedent due to the fact that there are only six companies in Mexico that have been the certified under this standard. Not only that, EGPM is **the only one of these companies that is part of the renewable energy sector**.
This prompted**Claudia Camarena** , Head of Health, Safety, Environment & Quality at Enel in Mexico, to say: “ _For us, safety has always been one of the fundamental pillars and that is why this certification fills us with pride, as it allows us to save lives and promote more responsible and safer behaviors as pedestrians, passengers or drivers. Today we see ourselves as one of the companies with the greatest attention to road safety in the world_.”
In 2017 we set out a road map for a Certified Road Safety Management System. In order to do so, it was necessary to analyze the data of the existing events and to strengthen a structure that was already firmly established. The company already had ISO 49000 certification. We focused on providing a **safe and healthy workplace**. These elements are present throughout our value chain, and that includes contractors who are involved in the construction and operation of all of our plants.
At the same time, we developed a unique initiative that consisted of an Internal Driving License with Points, which includes training and technical evaluation for all our collaborators in Mexico. The company only allows people who have this licence to use its parking facilities, drive company cars and rent vehicles for work purposes.
Furthermore, through the _Fleet Management_ initiative, we began the **monitoring of maximum vehicle speeds** , removing points from the internal license for each violation, speeding or malpractice. Today, this program has evolved into the 2.0 version, which includes a panic button and control of the use of the seat belt.
All these activities were taken into consideration during the certification process which, with the advent of the Covid-19 global pandemic, took about nine months. A special new remote protocol was required.
Worldwide, **only 547 companies are certified to the ISO 39001 standard** : 105 of them belong to the transport and logistics sector. Internationally, some 878,664 companies have ISO 9001 certification.
